{"key_event":"Salt March and Civil Disobedience","reasoning":"Civil society grew from already high nationalist mobilization into broader, more organized mass action through Congress networks, labor and peasant unions, women and students, and successful satyagraha, though communal/caste fragmentation and repression limited cohesion. The colonial state retained very strong administrative, coercive, fiscal, and constitutional capacity—using ordinances, arrests, bans, intelligence, and new financial institutions—but its legitimacy and revenue authority were visibly challenged by Depression-era protest and civil disobedience.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":7.3,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":8.5}
